rationale: Ground truth resolves this as 'duplicate of #4808'; Y explicitly reaches and justifies that same duplicate-closure conclusion, correctly notes its own checkout is the rebase branch (not upstream main) and can't verify main directly, and flags a real, well-grounded concern (#4808 shows mergedAt=null) plus a specific test-coverage gap. X instead asserts 'already fixed on main, closing' based on grepping what is actually the rebase agent's own dev/vllm-align checkout, misrepresenting it as upstream main state — a confident claim that diverges from the actual duplicate framing and risks giving the reporter false assurance if #4808 never merged. Y is more accurate, better grounded about the limits of its own evidence, and more complete in addressing follow-up risks.
